findings.description: Header names and titles layout changed from two columns to one.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, the Governor/Secretary and Director/Phone details are arranged in two parallel columns. In the HTML, they are stacked in a single left-aligned column.

findings.description: Loss of text formatting used for emphasis (italics and underlines).
findings.evidence: The PDF contains italicized sentences and underlined phrases for emphasis (e.g., 'not', 'unlicensed relative...'). The HTML renders these as plain text.

findings.description: Loss of text formatting used for emphasis (italics and underlines).
findings.evidence: The PDF contains italicized sentences and underlined phrases for emphasis (e.g., 'very', 'cannot', 'until September 30, 2002'). The HTML renders these as plain text.

findings.description: Loss of text formatting used for emphasis (italics and underlines).
findings.evidence: The PDF contains italicized sentences and underlined phrases for emphasis (e.g., 'cannot', 'trial home visit'). The HTML predominantly renders these as plain text.

findings.description: Image extraction error causing inverted colors.
findings.evidence: The signature image in the PDF is black ink on a white/transparent background. In the HTML, it appears as a negative image (white signature strokes on a solid black rectangle).

findings.description: Loss of bold italic formatting on document identifier.
findings.evidence: The document code 'CS-10-2002' is bold and italicized in the PDF, but rendered as plain text in the HTML (which flowed to the top of page 5's rendering).

findings.description: Loss of underline formatting in table cells.
findings.evidence: The PDF table uses underlines to emphasize key policy terms ('may', 'may not'). The HTML table renders these terms as plain text without underlines.
